source.title: Development of Trinitarian Creeds in Early Christianity
source.summary: Trinitarian formulas appear in the earliest Christian writings, including baptismal formulas in Matthew 28 (c. 80-90 AD), Paul's benedictions (c. 50s AD), and the Didache (c. 100 AD). The Apostles' Creed (c. 140 AD) and Nicene Creed (325 AD) formalized existing beliefs rather than inventing new ones. Archaeological evidence like the Rylands Papyrus P52 (c. 125 AD) containing John 1 shows early circulation of texts affirming Jesus' divine nature.
